safe/small-additive.description: Add health_check() method — zero-noise baseline. Any non-SAFE result is a bug.

safe/docs-only.description: README additions only, no code touched — confirms non-code changes don't false-positive.

safe/large-rename.description: Three class renames in auth.py — rename gap detector. False positive = L4 rename blind spot.

destructive/mass-deletion.description: Delete auth.py and database.py — canonical destructive payload.

destructive/april-2026-replica.description: Replica of April 2026 incident: benign commit message, catastrophic diff.

boundary/structural-threshold.description: 4 methods removed from auth.py (~23.5% structural deletion, just above 20% threshold).

semantic/no-description.description: No PR description — L5b returns UNVERIFIED but diff deletes database.py (DESTRUCTIVE).

semantic/honest-critical.description: Honest BREAKING CHANGE description of auth removal — L5b TRANSPARENT, overall DESTRUCTIVE.

multilang/structural-js-ts-go.description: Structural deletions across JS, TS, and Go — parser stress test.

adversarial/keyword-evasion.description: Deceptive description without benign trigger words — tests non-keyword evasion.

adversarial/rename-smuggling.description: auth.py renamed to identity.py with contents gutted — tests renamed-file blind spot.

adversarial/slow-deletion.description: 1 function removed from each of 5 files — distributed deletion evasion.

adversarial/addition-camouflage.description: Large api.js addition + auth.py deletion — addition camouflage pattern.

adversarial/nested-gutting.description: class Auth shell preserved, all methods deleted — tests L4 method tracking.

adversarial/threshold-gaming.description: Every metric tuned just below threshold — tests compound scoring defense.

adversarial/new-file-replacement.description: Delete auth.py, add auth_v2.py stub — delete+add replacement pattern.

adversarial/config-only-deletion.description: Config-only deletion (settings.yml, requirements.txt) — pure L2 signal test.

adversarial/unicode-payload.description: Hostile Unicode encoding in comments, minimal +4/-1 change — robustness/no-crash test.

workflow-security/base64-payload.description: Added workflow with base64-encoded payload piped to bash — L2c CRITICAL signal, score +5, immediate DESTRUCTIVE.

workflow-security/credential-harvest.description: Added workflow curling AWS metadata endpoint and grepping env for secrets — L2c CRITICAL signal, score +5.

workflow-security/dormant-trigger.description: Added workflow_dispatch trigger with shell exec payload — L2c HIGH (+3) plus L2b content scan also fires on curl|bash in the yml file (+4), total score 7 → DESTRUCTIVE. Defense-in-depth interaction, not a bug.

workflow-security/forged-bot-author.description: Added workflow configuring git identity as github-actions[bot] — L2c HIGH signal, score +3, CAUTION.

workflow-security/oidc-elevation.description: Added workflow with id-token: write and no legitimate OIDC consumer — L2c HIGH signal, score +3, CAUTION.

workflow-security/prt-write-permissions.description: Added pull_request_target workflow with contents: write + pull-requests: write — L2c CRITICAL escalation, score +5.

workflow-security/safe-clean-workflow.description: Added clean standard CI workflow — L2c baseline, no signals fire. Any non-SAFE result is a false positive bug.

adversarial/workflow-yaml-folded-block.description: Base64 payload split across YAML folded block lines — tests _normalize_yaml_content() bypass prevention.

adversarial/workflow-prt-only.description: pull_request_target with read-only permissions — HIGH signal only (no write escalation to CRITICAL).

adversarial/workflow-typosquatted-oidc.description: id-token: write with typosquatted consumer (aws-actions-unofficial/) — two-layer typosquat detection fires oidc_elevation_typosquatted (CRITICAL +5) → DESTRUCTIVE. Upgraded from CAUTION when typosquat detection was added as a distinct CRITICAL signal in v1.2.0.

adversarial/workflow-legitimate-oidc.description: id-token: write with legitimate aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials consumer — must not flag. False positive = exact-match regression.

adversarial/workflow-modified-poison.description: Existing workflow modified (change_type=M) to inject credential harvesting — tests that M-type diffs are scanned, not just A-type.

workflow-security/dependency-lock-tamper.description: Modifies workflow dependency lock file — validates interop with GitHub 2026 immutable dependency locking. Blocked pending API availability.

workflow-security/policy-bypass.description: Workflow redefines GITHUB_TOKEN scopes to escalate permissions — validates policy enforcement detection. Blocked pending API availability.

workflow-security/secret-exfiltration.description: Adds curl command to exfiltrate $SECRETS — complements GitHub 2026 native egress firewall as upstream block. Blocked pending API availability.

rta/push-rm-rf.description: RTA-04: Added workflow containing rm -rf — caught by _scan_added_file_content (.yml not excluded), score +2 → REVIEW. Inline 'on: workflow_dispatch' avoids L2c dormant-trigger pattern (requires newline form).

rta/schedule-curl-exfil.bypass_notes: RTA-05 partial: schedule trigger + curl POST body containing secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN with URL on next line. None of the credential_harvest patterns match: curl and secret are on different lines ([^\n]* stops at backslash-continuation newline); -d body not covered by -H/--header fix. Known gap — fix requires multiline-aware curl body pattern.
rta/schedule-curl-exfil.description: RTA-05 bypass: schedule cron + curl exfiltrating GITHUB_TOKEN via POST body (-d). URL on continuation line evades all current credential_harvest regexes. Expected SAFE until gap is closed.

rta/prt-untrusted-checkout.description: RTA-06: pull_request_target trigger with untrusted checkout (ref: head.sha). No explicit write permissions declared → HIGH signal only (+3), CAUTION. Mirrors adversarial/workflow-prt-only but as a red-team branch.

rta/github-env-injection.description: RTA-07 fixed: workflow injects PATH, LD_PRELOAD, and NODE_OPTIONS=--require into $GITHUB_ENV to poison all subsequent steps. Caught by _ACTIONS_GITHUB_ENV_INJECTION (Signal 7 added post-red-team), score +3 → CAUTION.

rta/variable-obfuscated-b64.description: RTA-08: Variable-indirected base64 payload — PAYLOAD=$(echo '...') then echo $PAYLOAD | base64 -d | bash. Obfuscation hides string but 'base64 -d | bash' literal still matches _ACTIONS_BASE64_PAYLOAD pattern 1, score +5 → DESTRUCTIVE.
